back_translation: Where do conflicts and quarrels among you come from? Do they not come from your desires that wage war within your own body parts?
You desire but do not get; you kill and envy, but cannot obtain what you want; you quarrel and fight; you do not have because you do not petition
You petition but do not receive, because you petition with wrong intentions, in order to use what you receive according to your own pleasures
You who are unfaithful to God, do you not know that being a friend with the world is being an enemy of God? Therefore, whoever chooses to be a friend of the world sets himself up as an enemy of God
Or do you think that the Scripture says in vain, “The spirit that He caused to dwell in us yearns with envy”?
But He grants grace even more. Therefore Scripture says, “God opposes the proud, but gives grace to the humble”
Therefore submit yourselves to God, but resist the devil, and he will flee from you
Draw near to God, and He will draw near to you. You sinners, wash your hands clean, and you double-minded, purify your hearts
Be in distress, mourn, and weep. Let your laughter be turned into mourning, and your joy into gloom
Humble yourselves before the Lord, and He will lift you up
Brothers, do not slander one another. Whoever slanders his brother or judges his brother, slanders the law and judges the law. If you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law, but a judge
There is only one Lawgiver and Judge, the One who is able to save and to destroy. But who are you to judge a neighbor?
Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a city, and stay there about a year, and do business and make a profit”
You do not even know what will happen tomorrow. What is your life like? You are like a mist that appears for a brief moment, and then fades away
Instead of saying that, you should say, “If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that”
But now you boast in your arrogance. All such boasting is evil
Therefore, whoever knows the good thing he should do, but does not do it, that person has sin
